columns.fragility_note: EE post-Soviet collapse creates extreme outliers pre-1995. Series nearly zero 1991-93.
columns.manipulability_note: Soviet vs. EU statistical standards discontinuity at 1991.

columns.fragility_note: Very sparse pre-2000. EE R&D data has structural break at EU accession 2004.
columns.manipulability_note: R&D expenditure classification changed after Frascati Manual 2015 revision.

columns.fragility_note: Available only post-EU accession (2004). Requires forward-fill for pre-accession period.
columns.manipulability_note: EE is a digital leader; ICT specialist rate is an outlier relative to EU average.

columns.fragility_note: Small absolute values; unit EUR millions. Currency conversion pre-Euro introduction.
columns.manipulability_note: Tax base reclassification coincides with EU accession harmonisation.

columns.fragility_note: Very small absolute values for EE. Series near-zero throughout; low symbolic diffusion signal.
columns.manipulability_note: Small-country effect: single large installation can dominate the series.

notes: EE meteo pilot (Estonia). Adapts the same Eurostat columns as other sectors to an EE context. Key structural breaks: Soviet collapse 1991, EU accession 2004, 2010 heat wave. O=industrial production (post-transition recovery proxy), R=R&D (adaptability post-accession), I=ICT integration, demand=env taxation, S=solar collector area. Robust_minmax normalisation clips the Soviet-era extreme dip.
